metadata.source: Kaggle (AI Health Guard, Disease and Symptoms, Medical Data) + GitHub (Medicare, Disease Prediction, ChatDoctor) + Medical References

Common Cold.description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract. Usually self-limiting within 7-10 days.
home_remedies: Drink warm fluids (ginger tea, honey lemon water)
Gargle with warm salt water 3-4 times daily
Get plenty of rest (8-10 hours sleep)
Stay hydrated (8-10 glasses of water daily)

Flu.description: Influenza is a contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses. More severe than common cold.

medications.name: Oseltamivir (Tamiflu)
medications.notes: Antiviral - best within 48 hours of onset

medications.name: Zinc lozenges
medications.notes: May reduce duration if started early

home_remedies: Stay in bed and rest completely for 3-5 days
Apply warm compress for body aches
Honey and lemon in warm water for throat

emergency_if: Difficulty breathing or shortness of breath

COVID-19.description: Coronavirus disease caused by SARS-CoV-2. Range from mild to severe respiratory illness.
home_remedies: Monitor oxygen levels with pulse oximeter (seek help if <92%)
Sleep on stomach (prone position) for breathing
Gargle with warm salt water for throat
COVID-19.doctor_type: General Practitioner / Pulmonologist / Infectious Disease Specialist
age_groups: All ages - higher risk in elderly

Diabetes.description: Chronic condition affecting how the body processes blood sugar (glucose). Type 1 or Type 2.
home_remedies: Monitor blood glucose daily (fasting and post-meal)
Check feet daily for cuts or sores

emergency_if: Signs of diabetic ketoacidosis (nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, fruity breath)

Hypertension.description: High blood pressure (≥130/80 mmHg) that can lead to heart disease, stroke, and kidney failure.

medications.name: Losartan
medications.notes: ARB - if ACE inhibitor causes cough

home_remedies: Monitor blood pressure daily at home

Migraine.description: Neurological condition with recurrent moderate to severe headaches, often one-sided.

medications.name: Sumatriptan
medications.notes: Triptan - best taken at first sign

home_remedies: Lie down in a dark, quiet room immediately
Apply cold or warm compress to head/neck
Drink caffeine (coffee/tea) at onset - may help

emergency_if: Headache with fever and stiff neck
Headache with confusion, weakness, or vision loss

Asthma.description: Chronic respiratory condition with airway inflammation and constriction, causing breathing difficulty.
home_remedies: Use peak flow meter daily to monitor lung function
Use air purifier with HEPA filter

eat: Foods rich in vitamin C (citrus, bell peppers)

emergency_if: Rescue inhaler not helping after 15 minutes
Chest feels tight and won't relax

Pneumonia.description: Infection causing inflammation in air sacs of the lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.

medications.name: Azithromycin
medications.notes: For atypical pneumonia or penicillin allergy

home_remedies: Drink fluids to thin mucus (warm water, herbal tea)
Deep breathing exercises every 2 hours while awake

Appendicitis.description: Inflammation of the appendix. Medical emergency requiring prompt surgical removal.
home_remedies: DO NOT attempt home treatment - this is a medical emergency
Do NOT eat or drink (nil per os) if appendicitis suspected
Do NOT take laxatives or enemas
Do NOT apply heat to abdomen

eat: Post-surgery: clear liquids, then soft foods

emergency_if: Severe abdominal pain especially lower right

Depression.description: Mood disorder causing persistent sadness and loss of interest, affecting daily functioning.

medications.name: Bupropion (NDRI)
medications.notes: If sexual side effects from SSRIs

Anxiety Disorder.description: Mental health condition with excessive and persistent worry, fear, and nervousness.

see_doctor_if: Anxiety interfering with daily life >2 weeks
emergency_if: First panic attack (may mimic heart attack)

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of stomach and intestines causing diarrhea and vomiting. Often viral (stomach flu).
home_remedies: Drink ORS or homemade solution (1L water + 6tsp sugar + 0.5tsp salt)
Eat BRAT diet (Bananas, Rice, Applesauce, Toast)
Rest and avoid solid food for 12-24 hours

emergency_if: Severe dehydration (dry mouth, no urine, dizziness)

age_groups: All ages - higher risk in children and elderly

Anemia.description: Condition characterized by lack of healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to tissues.

medications.name: Ferrous sulfate (Iron supplement)
medications.notes: Take with vitamin C for absorption

home_remedies: Eat iron-rich foods (red meat, spinach, lentils)
Avoid tea/coffee 1 hour before and after meals
Pair iron with vitamin C foods

see_doctor_if: Shortness of breath with mild activity

Arthritis.description: Inflammation of one or more joints causing pain and stiffness that can worsen with age.

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.notes: For pain if NSAIDs not suitable

home_remedies: Weight management to reduce joint stress

Bronchitis.description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes causing cough and mucus production. Can be acute or chronic.
home_remedies: Drink plenty of fluids to thin mucus
Use humidifier to add moisture to air
Gargle warm salt water for sore throat

Dengue.description: Mosquito-borne viral infection causing fever and severe joint/muscle pain. Can develop into severe dengue (dengue hemorrhagic fever).

medications.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
medications.notes: For fever and pain - AVOID NSAIDs

home_remedies: Drink plenty of fluids (coconut water, oral rehydration)
Papaya leaf extract may help platelets (consult doctor)
precautions: Seek immediate care if symptoms worsen

Food Poisoning.description: Illness caused by eating contaminated food or drink. Symptoms typically appear within hours to days.

medications.name: Loperamide
medications.notes: NOT for bloody diarrhea or high fever

home_remedies: Sip clear fluids frequently (water, clear broths, electrolyte drinks)
Eat BRAT diet (Bananas, Rice, Applesauce, Toast)

Gallstones.description: Hardened deposits in the gallbladder that can block bile ducts and cause severe pain and complications.
home_remedies: Apply warm compress to abdomen during pain
Apple cider vinegar (1 tbsp in water) may help

Hepatitis A.description: Viral liver infection spread through contaminated food/water. Usually self-limiting with lifelong immunity.

medications.name: Supportive care only
medications.notes: No specific antiviral - rest and hydration

medications.name: Paracetamol
medications.notes: For fever - avoid if liver severely affected

risk_factors: Men who have sex with men

Hepatitis B.description: Viral liver infection transmitted through blood and bodily fluids. Can become chronic and lead to cirrhosis.

Hepatitis C.description: Viral infection causing liver inflammation, often chronic. Now highly curable with modern antivirals.
home_remedies: Get vaccinated for Hepatitis A and B
precautions: Get vaccinated for Hep A and B

Hyperthyroidism.description: Overactive thyroid gland producing excessive thyroid hormones, accelerating metabolism.

medications.name: Propranolol
medications.notes: Beta-blocker for rapid heart rate and tremor

eat: Cruciferous vegetables (may help reduce thyroid hormone)

see_doctor_if: Heat intolerance interfering with daily life
emergency_if: Thyroid storm: fever >38.5°C, rapid heart rate, agitation, confusion

Hypothyroidism.description: Underactive thyroid gland not producing enough thyroid hormones, slowing metabolism.

medications.name: Liothyronine (T3)
medications.notes: T3 hormone - sometimes added to T4

home_remedies: Take medication on empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast

emergency_if: Myxedema coma: severe cold intolerance, confusion, lethargy

precautions: Take medication consistently at same time

Jaundice.description: Yellow discoloration of skin and eyes caused by high bilirubin levels, a symptom of underlying liver or blood disorder.

medications.name: Treat underlying cause
medications.notes: Jaundice is a symptom, not a disease - treat cause

medications.name: Paracetamol
medications.notes: For fever - avoid if liver disease severe

medications.name: Vitamin K
medications.notes: If clotting problems from liver dysfunction

Kidney Stones.description: Hard mineral and salt deposits forming in kidneys, causing severe pain when passing through urinary tract.

medications.name: Tamsulosin (alpha-blocker)
medications.notes: Helps relax ureter to pass stone

home_remedies: Strain urine to catch stone for analysis
Gentle movement/walking may help pass stone

see_doctor_if: Pain not controlled with OTC meds

Malaria.description: Life-threatening mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. Requires prompt treatment.
home_remedies: Sponge with cool water for fever
precautions: Take prophylaxis if traveling to endemic area

Osteoporosis.description: Bone disease causing bones to become brittle and fragile, increasing fracture risk, especially in older adults.

medications.name: Alendronate (Fosamax)
medications.notes: Bisphosphonate - take on empty stomach with full glass water

home_remedies: Get adequate sunlight for vitamin D

emergency_if: Spinal compression fracture with nerve symptoms

Tuberculosis.description: Bacterial infection primarily affecting the lungs, requiring prolonged antibiotic treatment. Can be fatal if untreated.
home_remedies: Complete FULL treatment course (never stop early)

avoid: Alcohol (interferes with treatment and damages liver)

emergency_if: Massive hemoptysis (coughing large amounts of blood)

Typhoid.description: Bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, spread through contaminated food and water. Can be life-threatening.

medications.name: Azithromycin
medications.notes: First-line treatment, especially for drug-resistant strains

medications.name: Paracetamol
medications.notes: For fever - never give aspirin (risk of bleeding)

see_doctor_if: Symptoms not improving after 48 hours of antibiotics

precautions: Avoid street food in endemic areas
risk_factors: Travel to South Asia, Africa, Latin America

Urinary Tract Infection.description: Infection in any part of the urinary system (kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra). More common in women.

medications.name: Phenazopyridine (Pyridium)
medications.notes: For pain relief only - turns urine orange

doctor_specialties.General Practitioner (GP): First point of contact for general health issues
doctor_specialties.Neurologist: Brain, spine, and nervous system conditions
doctor_specialties.Urologist: Urinary system and male reproductive organs
